cross_scene_arcs.title: The Descent into Coercive Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gabriel's relationship with Stanley evolves from manipulative recruitment to overt physical and psychological domination, using technology, threats, and emotional blackmail to enforce compliance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stanley is coerced into cooperation through a mix of financial incentive and veiled threats concerning his daughter.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Stanley is broken emotionally and forced into compliance through the threat of his daughter's death, marking the peak of Gabriel's coercive control.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Stanley's long-term loyalty remains uncertain, and his potential for rebellion or sabotage persists despite apparent submission.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Rogue Operative to Autonomous Warlord
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gabriel transitions from a government-sanctioned agent to a self-justified revolutionary, rejecting institutional authority and redefining himself as a necessary extremist fighting for American ideals.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gabriel disobeys direct orders, severing ties with official command and declaring independence from political oversight.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Gabriel executes a high-ranking official and frames his actions as righteous, positioning himself as a mythic, autonomous force beyond the law.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The legitimacy of Gabriel's self-appointed mission remains contested, and his ideological isolation raises questions about his long-term viability.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Unraveling of Absolute Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gabriel establishes total dominance over the bank and hostages through escalating threats, only to face systemic and human resistance that undermines his control and forces violent escalation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gabriel neutralizes FBI intervention by revealing explosive implants in hostages, asserting unchallengeable authority.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Despite securing the money transfer, Gabriel's control collapses due to technical failure and resistance, forcing him to resort to murder and last-ditch threats.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The source of the system failure and whether it was an inside sabotage remain unknown, suggesting vulnerabilities in Gabriel's operation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Hidden Fractures Within the Inner Circle
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gabriel's operation is compromised from within, as surveillance, hidden identities, and shifting loyalties create internal conflict that threatens the mission's integrity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ginger is revealed to be wired, suggesting possible betrayal or external surveillance within Gabriel's trusted circle.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ginger is executed by Gabriel to maintain control, eliminating the immediate threat but confirming the presence of deep internal instability.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Ginger's true allegiance and the identity of the party behind the wire remain unknown, leaving open the possibility of further embedded threats.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Myth and Fall of Gabriel Shear
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gabriel is constructed as an untouchable, mythic antagonist—only to be abruptly killed, shattering his aura of invincibility and shifting the narrative into a mystery-driven investigation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gabriel is introduced with cinematic grandeur, establishing him as a powerful, otherworldly figure operating beyond normal constraints.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Gabriel is found dead in his own wine cooler, his body a symbol of vulnerability and the collapse of his carefully constructed control.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The identity and motive of Gabriel's killer remain unknown, casting doubt on who truly held power in the operation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Catalyst of Reconnection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ginger initiates contact with Stanley to pull him out of isolation, gradually shifting from professional intermediary to emotional provocateur by revealing she contacted his estranged daughter, Holly. Her methods escalate from assertive to invasive, culminating in a physical ejection that marks the collapse of their initial rapport.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ginger arrives as a confident outsider aiming to reestablish contact and influence Stanley's reentry into society.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Their alliance fractures after Ginger oversteps by forcing emotional confrontation, resulting in her violent expulsion.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Stanley's capacity for reconciliation remains uncertain, and Ginger's role as helper or manipulator is still ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Ally to Architect of Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After being rejected, Ginger reasserts dominance by offering transformative resources—legal funding and access to Gabriel—positioning herself as indispensable. She regains control over Stanley's trajectory, shifting from intruder to gatekeeper of his only viable path forward.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ginger returns with high-value leverage, reframing herself as a powerful enabler in Stanley's custody battle.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She secures Stanley's continued dependence, even after he considers abandoning the mission, by discrediting alternatives and reinforcing her authority.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her true motivations and the cost of her assistance remain hidden, casting doubt on whether she empowers or exploits him.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Unraveling of Deception
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stanley discovers Ginger is wired and armed, triggering a confrontation that exposes her as an operative. Though she attempts damage control and emotional reconnection, her cover and credibility begin to crumble under scrutiny, creating a rift between personal loyalty and professional deception.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ginger's covert status is exposed, placing her in immediate danger and forcing her into survival mode.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Her emotional appeal is met with silence and sarcasm, signaling a breakdown in trust despite Stanley's act of protecting her.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Ginger can maintain her cover or if her dual identity will ultimately destroy her relationship with Stanley.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mission Over Man: The Betrayal of Trust
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ginger transitions from emotional supporter to mission-driven agent, insisting Stanley complete the worm despite the risks. Her commitment leads to her apparent death—a staged execution used to manipulate Stanley—after which doubts emerge about her survival, reframing her sacrifice as potential strategy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ginger publicly aligns with Stanley emotionally, reinforcing his importance to the mission.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She is seemingly killed to coerce Stanley into compliance, but her body vanishes, suggesting the possibility of survival and calculated deception.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Ginger truly died or faked her death to escape, and what role she may still play in the larger operation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Phantom Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After being presumed dead, Ginger resurfaces through identity exposure and financial action—first revealing her DEA status, then appearing (or being impersonated) in Monte Carlo to access offshore funds. This arc blurs truth and illusion, suggesting Ginger may have orchestrated her disappearance as part of a deeper game.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ginger reveals her true identity as a DEA agent, attempting to reclaim authority over the mission.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She vanishes again, this time fleeing Monte Carlo with a mysterious ally after a failed transfer, now resembling a fugitive rather than a law enforcement officer.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Ginger is acting as a rogue agent, a double operative, or a victim of identity exploitation remains unknown, leaving her ultimate allegiance and fate in question.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Isolation to Reluctant Reengagement
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stanley transitions from self-imposed isolation and resistance to returning to hacking, triggered by emotional leverage involving his daughter and professional validation under duress, ultimately committing to an 'impossible' technical task for personal redemption.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stanley resists re-engaging with his hacking past, citing legal consequences and disconnection from that world.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Stanley commits to building a multi-headed worm for Gabriel, motivated by the promise of regaining custody of his daughter.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His long-term loyalty and true intentions remain ambiguous, with emotional manipulation driving his involvement.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Alliance with Ginger
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stanley's relationship with Ginger deteriorates from tense collaboration to open confrontation and collapse as he uncovers her deception, leading to a breakdown in trust and his decision to abandon the mission.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stanley suspects Ginger is wired and confronts her, initiating a crisis of trust.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The alliance collapses as Stanley rejects Ginger and the mission, choosing to return to Roberts.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Ginger's true allegiance and the identity of 'he' who will kill her remain unknown, leaving Stanley vulnerable and emotionally adrift.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Awakening and Defiance of Gabriel
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stanley evolves from coerced participant to moral dissenter, rejecting Gabriel's ideological justifications for violence and ultimately attempting to stop the terrorist operation, culminating in a failed confrontation on the rooftop.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stanley remains focused on his daughter but begins questioning Gabriel's motives and the legitimacy of his cause.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Stanley openly defies Gabriel and tries to stop the escape, though he fails to prevent the virus from being taken.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his moral clarity, Stanley is powerless to stop Gabriel, and the threat of the virus remains active.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into Fugitive Survival
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stanley's status shifts from a marginalized hacker to a fugitive on the run after a violent escape, enduring physical trauma and emotional devastation as he transitions from seeking custody to surviving and protecting his daughter.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stanley is intercepted by federal agents during a custody visit, forcing him into violent flight.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He survives a near-fatal fall and learns of his daughter's abduction after finding his ex-wife and her partner murdered.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Stanley is physically and emotionally shattered, with no safe path forward and his daughter still in danger.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Illusion of Death and Hidden Truths
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stanley experiences and later investigates staged deaths—first Gabriel's apparent corpse, then Ginger's missing body—triggering a pattern of uncovering deception and illusion that challenges his perception of reality and fuels his suspicion of a larger conspiracy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stanley discovers what appears to be Gabriel's dead body, triggering fear and flight.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He begins to suspect that deaths are faked, linking the disappearances to Houdini-style illusions and questioning the official narrative.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The truth behind the staged deaths and the extent of the conspiracy remain hidden, leaving Stanley in a state of cognitive dissonance and unresolved danger.
